narration: Your brain makes 35,000 decisions every single day. By 4 PM it's running on fumes — and that's quietly costing you money.
visual: Person silhouette with a glowing brain above them. A battery gauge labeled DECISION FUEL drains from 100% to nearly empty. A counter ticks up to 35,000.

narration: Scientists call it decision fatigue. Every choice you make burns through your mental fuel — and once it's gone, your brain starts cutting corners on everything.
visual: Clock hands sweep from 9 AM to 4 PM. An energy bar drains alongside the clock. Text: EACH CHOICE BURNS MENTAL FUEL.

narration: An Israeli study tracked judges — in the morning they approved parole 65% of the time. Just before lunch? Twenty percent. Same judge, same crime, different hour.
visual: Gavel icon springs in. Two side-by-side bars animate upward: 65% tall green bar labeled MORNING and 20% short red bar labeled PRE-LUNCH.

narration: Retailers figured this out decades ago. Sale emails hit at 4 and 5 PM on purpose. Flash deals, one-click checkout — all engineered for your exhausted brain.
visual: Phone screen showing 4:00 PM. Five notification banners appear one by one: SALE ENDS TONIGHT, 50% OFF, YOUR CART IS WAITING, FLASH DEAL, JUST FOR YOU 40% OFF.

narration: The average American spends 22% more on purchases made after 4 PM. Run that math — it's $1,400 drained from your wallet every single year.
visual: Stack of dollar bills fades away one by one. A large counter animates from $0 up to $1,400. Text underneath: DRAINED EVERY YEAR.

narration: The fix is dead simple. Make your big money decisions before 10 AM when your brain is fresh. Your morning self is genuinely your richest self.
visual: Sunrise SVG with golden rays rotating slowly. Coffee cup beside it. Three checklist items check off: CHECK BALANCES BEFORE 9 AM, BIG BUYS MORNING ONLY, NO SHOPPING APPS AFTER 4 PM.
